chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يَتْرُكُ الشَّيْ‏ءَ الْقَلِيلَ وَ عَلَيْهِ دَيْنٌ أَكْثَرُ مِنْهُ وَ لَهُ عِيَالٌ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 29 – The man leaves something little, and upon him is a debt more than it, and for him are dependants

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/73)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/73)
text: هُ سُئِلَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ يَمُوتُ وَ يَتْرُكُ عِيَالًا وَ عَلَيْهِ دَيْنٌ أَ يُنْفِقُ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنْ مَالِهِ قَالَ إِنِ اسْتَيْقَنَ أَنَّ الدَّيْنَ الَّذِي عَلَيْهِ يُحِيطُ بِجَمِيعِ الْمَالِ فَلَا يُنْفِقُ عَلَيْهِمْ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يَسْتَيْقِنْ فَلْيُنْفِقْ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنْ وَسَطِ الْمَالِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Abu Nast, by a chain of his that,

‘He<sup>asws</sup> was asked about a man who died and left dependents, and upon him was a debt. Can he spend upon them from his wealth?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If he is convinced that the debt which is upon him encompasses the entirety of the wealth, so he should not spend upon them, and if he is not convinced, so let him spend over them from the middle of the wealth’.
en.sarwar: 1. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn abu Nasr Through the same chain of narrators as that of the previous Hadith the following is narrated: “He once asked him (the Imam); ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who dies leaving behind dependents and debts; if anything can be spent from the legacy for the dependents. He (the Imam) said, ‘If it is certain that his legacy covers the debt, then nothing can be spent for his dependents but if it is certain that the legacy cannot cover the debts, then it is permissible to spend thereof for the dependents.’”
